English.purpose: This document defines the automated workflow for managing stale issues and pull requests within the ArchSpine repository. It ensures that inactive items are systematically flagged and eventually closed, keeping the repository clean and focused.
English.context_and_audience: Repository maintainers and contributors who need to understand how long-dormant issues and PRs are handled automatically. This policy runs on a daily schedule and applies to all open issues and PRs unless they carry exempt labels.
key_takeaways: Issues and pull requests are marked 'stale' after 60 days of inactivity.
After being marked stale, they are closed automatically in 14 more days unless activity resumes.
Items labeled 'security' or 'on-deck' are exempt from this automated triage.
The workflow is defined as a GitHub Actions cron job, running daily at 1:30 UTC.
